items.title: Messi and Ronaldo Are Building Tech Portfolios. Mo Salah Is Playing a Different Game
items.summary: Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo are betting on AI, health tech, and startups. Mohamed Salah is taking a more traditional route beyond football.

items.title: The FTC Settlement With John Deere Is a Huge Win for the Right-to-Repair Movement
items.summary: After more than a decade of pushback, farmers and repair advocates have won access to equipment and services John Deere had long kept under its control.

items.title: I Built a Self-Improving AI, and So Can You
items.summary: Experiments in using AI to build AI show that the future doesn’t just belong to the frontier labs.

items.title: Lovable reportedly in talks to double its valuation to $13.2B
items.summary: The $300 million round is expected to be led by Menlo Ventures, Sifted reported.

items.title: Feds demand autonomous vehicle companies stop interfering with first responders
items.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said emergency scenes are not "edge cases."

items.title: Google’s deepfake detector system used to debunk McConnell hoax pic
items.summary: Earlier this week, a picture seemed to show Kentucky Senator Mitch McConnell covered in tubes in a hospital bed in a state of extreme distress. It turned out to be an AI-generated fake.

items.title: US launches more strikes on Iran with blasts reported in south of country
items.summary: Trump says the attacks are "retribution for yesterday's bombing of ships by Iran". Iran earlier promised strong retaliation.

items.title: US strikes target Iranian military boats
items.summary: The US has launched the strikes on Iran in response to attacks on three oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Ukraine to get licence to produce Patriot missiles, Trump says
items.summary: Although the Patriots are highly effective against Russia's ballistic missiles, they also have lengthy production times.

items.title: Michael Burry bets on sportsbooks DraftKings and Flutter, sees prediction markets curbed by regulation
items.summary: The investor believes regulators will eventually crack down on prediction markets after competition from the upstarts pressured the stocks of sportsbooks.

items.title: Fed officials were split on direction of interest rates at last meeting, minutes show
items.summary: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released minutes from its June 16-17 meeting.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal until 2027 after latest setback, Kalshi traders predict
items.summary: Speculators now see just a 43% chance that traffic flows return to normal by Dec. 1.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Lawmakers demand answers after fatal ICE shooting in Texas
items.summary: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill are calling for an investigation after an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer fatally shot a Mexican immigrant in Houston on Tuesday. An ICE spokesperson said officers with the agency had tried to conduct a vehicle stop as part of a targeted operation. The driver, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, did not follow…

items.title: Republicans prepare $8 million ad campaign against possible Platner replacement
items.summary: Republicans are preparing to launch an $8 million ad blitz against the candidate selected to replace Maine Senate Democratic nominee Graham Platner in the wake of sexual assault allegations.  The Super PAC Pine Tree Results spent $2 million in May on YouTube and television ads bashing Platner and still has $8 million left to fight…

items.title: Iran ceasefire’s end drives up oil prices and uncertainty
items.summary: The end of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is driving oil prices higher and fueling uncertainty over future energy prices. On Tuesday and again Wednesday, the U.S. said it was striking Iran in retaliation for attacks on three commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump, speaking at the NATO summit in Turkey, declared that the ceasefire that ended…

items.title: Bank of America warns America now has 2 economies
items.summary: Bank of America warns America now has 2 economies

items.title: Volatility Looks Dead. That Means This ETF Could Be Getting Ready to Soar.
items.summary: Volatility Looks Dead. That Means This ETF Could Be Getting Ready to Soar.

items.title: Consumers dread rising healthcare, rent costs: New York Fed
items.summary: Consumers dread rising healthcare, rent costs: New York Fed

items.title: New weight loss pill beats oral Ozempic in major trial
items.summary: A new once-daily weight-loss pill called orforglipron delivered better weight loss and blood sugar improvements than the leading oral semaglutide in a major clinical trial. The tablet could offer a more convenient alternative to injectable drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy because it doesn't require refrigeration or special timing with meals. It's also cheaper to manufacture, which could expand access globally.

items.title: Ancient DNA reveals the mysterious collapse of Europe's megalith builders
items.summary: DNA from a 5,000-year-old French megalithic tomb reveals that the people buried before and after a population collapse were genetically unrelated, pointing to a major migration after a devastating crisis. The shift coincided with new social traditions and the disappearance of the communities that built Europe's giant stone monuments.

items.title: Incredible new material makes heat programmable
items.summary: A newly developed material can control and "program" heat, allowing it to direct thermal radiation, switch modes, and remember its settings without continuous power. The innovation could lead to smarter infrared sensors, better energy technologies, and memory devices that use light and heat instead of electrical charges.

items.title: Can geoengineering blunt El Niño’s fury?
items.summary: Marine cloud brightening could cool part of the Pacific and weaken extreme El Niños, simulations suggest. But the approach could have risks.

items.title: A shoebox-sized satellite could expose hidden nuclear weapons in space
items.summary: There’s never been a good method to check for violations of the Outer Space Treaty’s prohibition of nuclear weapons in space.

items.title: AI tools meant to vet science are surprisingly easy to fool
items.summary: The gold standard of scientific review, peer review by researchers’ colleagues, is in crisis. AI might offer a solution but has problems of its own.
